title: Anxiety Therapy in Leesburg, Virginia
subtitle: When Anxiety Shows Up as Overthinking and Perfectionism

sections.title: When Anxiety Shows Up as Overthinking and Perfectionism
sections.body:
Many adults experience anxiety not as panic, but as constant mental noise. Overthinking. Second-guessing. A feeling of pressure to get things right and a difficulty in ever really resting.

Anxiety can show up as perfectionism, rumination, or a sense that your mind never really shuts off. You might appear high functioning on the outside while feeling tense, driven, or exhausted on the inside.

Often, these patterns are not flaws to fix, but adaptations that once helped you cope. Therapy offers a space to understand where these patterns came from and how to relate to them differently.

sections.title: Anxiety Therapy that looks beneath the surface.
sections.body:
In my work with adults, anxiety is approached with curiosity rather than judgment. Instead of focusing only on symptom reduction, we look at the underlying experiences, nervous system responses, and relational patterns that keep anxiety in place. 

This may include exploring how early experiences shaped your sense of safety, how your body responds to stress, or how self-criticism and control developed as protective strategies. Therapy becomes less about forcing change and more about creating conditions for steadiness, flexibility, and self-trust. Sessions are collaborative and paced with care. We move in a way that respects your nervous system rather than pushing it. Change is possible.

sections.title: For the high-achiever who feels like you're constantly "on":
sections.body: Managing a racing mind while trying to meet the relentless pressure to be enough, you are likely carrying a weight that wasn't yours to hold alone. I provide specialized anxiety and perfectionism therapy in Leesburg for individuals who are tired of just "holding it together." Together, we can move beyond the surface-level stress and unearth the roots of these patterns, helping your nervous system finally find a sense of genuine ease.

sections.title: How I Help Beyond "Just Talking"
sections.body: Most high-achievers have already tried to logic their way out of anxiety. You’ve read the books, you’ve tried the deep breathing, and you know, intellectually, that you are enough. But your nervous system hasn't received the memo. In our work together, we move past the surface-level strategies and get to the root of the "never enough" narrative using two powerful, evidence-based approaches:

sections.title: Internal Family Systems (IFS): Meeting the Perfectionist
sections.body: Perfectionism isn't a character flaw; it’s a protective part of you that likely works very hard to keep you safe, successful, and/or accepted. Using an IFS-informed approach, we won't try to fix or get rid of this part. Instead, we’ll get curious about it. By understanding what this perfectionist part is afraid would happen if it let go of the reins, we can help it soften, allowing your true, authentic self to lead.

sections.title: EMDR: Rewiring the "Not Enough" Loop
sections.body: Anxiety and perfectionism are often stored in the body as "stuck" memories or beliefs from the past. EMDR (Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing) helps your brain reprocess those experiences so they no longer trigger a fight-or-flight response in your present life. This isn't just about managing symptoms; it’s about changing how your brain and body react to pressure, so ease can become your new baseline.

sections.title: Imagine being able to:
items: Safely express your thoughts, feelings, and experiences without judgment
Identify and overcome barriers to self-care so that you can be cared for
Move away from shame and isolation and toward connection and support
